labelingRules: Use 'Review' in visible UI copy before 'Questionnaire' unless the user is editing source data.
Use status labels Cited, Needs Review, Blocked, Published consistently.
Use Missing proof and Owner needed for blocked states.
Avoid generic compliance jargon when a workflow term exists.
informationArchitecture.validationMethod: 10-second job test, primary-action test, logo-swap test, and keyboard path review on every primary screen.

guardrails: No answer without citation can be marked complete.
Blocked questions must expose a direct remediation CTA.
Review Queue is the default post-login route.

screenSpecs.label: Login
screenSpecs.job: Let the user enter the workspace quickly.
screenSpecs.firstNoticedElement: Proofline title and concise product promise
screenSpecs.layoutGrammar: Centered split intro with left promise block and right auth card

screenSpecs.label: Review Queue
screenSpecs.job: Let the operator choose the next buyer review to process.
screenSpecs.firstNoticedElement: Status-weighted review rows with Cited, Needs Review, and Blocked counts
screenSpecs.layoutGrammar: Nav rail plus dense central review list with inline row actions

screenSpecs.label: Review Room
screenSpecs.job: Resolve one buyer review until it can be published.
screenSpecs.firstNoticedElement: Coverage header showing cited, needs review, and blocked counts
screenSpecs.layoutGrammar: Three-column desk with question rail, document canvas, and evidence ledger

screenSpecs.label: Systems
screenSpecs.job: Maintain the minimal AI system records needed for review answers.
screenSpecs.firstNoticedElement: System roster with owner and linked evidence count

screenSpecs.label: Evidence
screenSpecs.job: Add, edit, and link reusable proof items.
screenSpecs.firstNoticedElement: Evidence rows grouped by linked system and evidence strength
screenSpecs.layoutGrammar: Dense evidence list with metadata side panel

screenSpecs.label: Buyer Packet
screenSpecs.job: Present a buyer-facing proof packet with readable cited answers and publication context.
screenSpecs.firstNoticedElement: Packet header with review name, status, and generated timestamp
screenSpecs.layoutGrammar: Editorial document with anchored table of contents and cited sections

componentBindings.name: StatusChip
componentBindings.purpose: Consistent state language with icon + text

componentBindings.name: DrawerEditor
componentBindings.purpose: Inline create/edit flows for systems and evidence

componentBindings.name: CoverageHeader
componentBindings.purpose: Custom wrapper for review room summary

states.loading: Skeleton rows match final row density
states.empty: Headline plus Seed Demo Review action
states.error: Inline error block with retry action
states.loaded: Rows show status counts and due date

states.loading: Question rail, editor, and evidence ledger skeletons
states.blocked: Missing proof or Owner needed message with direct remediation CTA
states.editing: Save Answer available, publish disabled when uncited answers remain
states.publish_ready: All required questions cited or acknowledged; publish enabled
states.publish_error: Publish banner explains why packet could not be issued

states.empty: No systems yet plus New System CTA
No evidence yet plus Add Evidence CTA

states.published: Readable packet with proof summary and cited sections
states.stale: Packet marked outdated if review changed after publication
states.error: Packet unavailable block with back-to-review action

visualDirection.intent: Make the app feel like a buyer review desk rather than a generic analytics dashboard.
layoutGrammar: single dominant work surface per screen
dense rows over decorative card grids
review room center canvas reads like a document under review
packet page reads like an editorial proof packet

styleAnchors: status chips with icon plus text
styleConstraints: No texture heavy enough to hurt readability.
Do not ship default library radius/shadow without token changes.

accessibilityContract.contrast: Light theme must maintain high text contrast and accessible status contrast.
accessibilityContract.focus: All interactive elements use visible focus-visible ring with focus token.
accessibilityContract.statusSemantics: Status uses icon plus text; color-only communication is forbidden.
accessibilityContract.keyboardNav: Primary nav, question rail, row actions, and drawer triggers must be keyboard reachable.
accessibilityContract.packetSemantics: Packet uses semantic headings, lists, and section landmarks.
accessibilityContract.reducedMotion: Respect prefers-reduced-motion by removing non-essential transitions.

contentRules: Prefer operator language over abstract compliance jargon.
Use concise trust language: Evidence, Owner, Packet, Cited, Blocked.
Blocked and error states must use product-specific copy, not generic alert text.
Avoid hype language in product UI.
motionRules: Use 140-220ms transitions for hover, focus, drawer, and panel state shifts.
Publish success can highlight state but should avoid celebratory motion.
Loading states should prefer skeletons over spinners.

buildConstraints.componentReuseBudget: Reuse shadcn primitives for most surfaces; allow 2-3 custom wrappers only.
buildConstraints.motionBudget: Minimal; no animation libraries required for v1.
driftSentinels: If the first authenticated screen becomes a metric dashboard, reject the build.
If the review room center panel does not look like a working document, reject the build.
If packet view looks like an export modal instead of buyer-facing proof, reject the build.
